note: A very uncertain analysis based on the possible source location (minor dimming) and on imagery from one coronagraph only (SOHO) because the CME is covered by a data gap in STEREO A. The CME seems to deflect northward from the location of the source.

An extremely approximate analysis based on a fit with two coronagraphs in swpc_cat coronagraph. The fit can be made with much lower and much higher longitudes (130 to 160 degrees, probably even wider range).

These parameters are based on the best fit of the leading edge of the CME with the closest fit to the right hand boundary (lower side of CME front). There is some uncertainty with longitude due to limited stereoscopic viewpoints of the source.

Measurement of faint leading edge now that more imagery has become available. Leading edge becomes too diffuse to track after only about 12 frames in SOHO LASCO C3. Tricky to track leading edge in STEREO A COR2 after frame 2024-03-28T23:38Z due to change in cadence of difference imagery frames to a longer cadence.
Remeasurement using additional imagery in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. This CME was faint, so limited imagery was available for this measurement.
Measurement using best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT, as well as location of source eruption.

Measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only due to the CME's faintness in STEREO A COR2 difference imagery. There is some uncertainty in these parameters because measurement attempts that included STEREO A COR2 did not yield a good fit between SOHO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is visible beyond the northern limb and a precise source location is unknown, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Due to an uncertain/far-sided source, a precise source location is unknown. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.

Measurement taken at W90, not as a plane of sky, as an approximation of the source which is on or near the west limb. Using both SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A imagery, corroborating the source being near the limb with a good triangulation.

Measurement made with only STEREO COR2A imagery as SOHO LASCO C2/C3 have yet to backfill, expect further analysis. Approximate source longitude of E115 made through a visual fit, longitude range could be from E110-E120, definitely sourced on the far-side in EUV imagery.

Very uncertain measurement based on the assumption that the source is Active Region 3615 (at ~140 deg longitude, behind the limb) and on one-coronagraph (STEREO A) analysis in swpc_cat. No LASCO imagery is available for this CME b/c data was not backfilled due to cron job failure.
Analysis based on a fit in swpc_cat with 2 coronagraphs and on the location of AR 3615.

Measurement of faint leading edge using estimated location of candidate source AR 3615 at W150 and best fit between coronagraphs. Visible for only three frames of STEREO A COR2 difference imagery. Data gap in STEREO A COR2 from 2024-04-03T18:53Z to 2024-04-04T02:53Z.
Approximate analysis with only LASCO coronagraph based on the assumption that the source of the CME is AR 3615.
Analysis based on fit with two coronagraphs with swpc_cat and corroborated by backsided location of Active Region 3615.
Approximate analysis based on a good fit in swpc_cat with two coronagraphs and fitting well the location of AR 3615.
Approximate analysis based on a good fit with two coronagraphs in swpc_cat and confirmed by location of AR 3615 at the time.
Approximate analysis based on best fit in swpc_cat tool with two coronagraphs, with good correspondence with the backsided location of AR 3615.
Analysis based on fit with two coronagraphs with swpc_cat and corroborated by backsided location of Active Region 3615.
Analysis based solely on the fit with two coronagraphs. The resulting longitude corresponds well to the longitude of an active region seen on the far side.

Measurement based on the approximate source location of Active Region 13615. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery there is no stereoscopic viewpoint available for this event. The measured parameters may therefore vary as a result.

Preliminary measurement based on the approximate longitude of former Active Region 13615. This measurement uses only a few available frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery as there are large data gaps in both coronagraphs preventing much of this event from being observed. The measured parameters may vary as a result.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on the approximate longitude of former Active Region 13615. This event becomes too faint to measure after a few frames and there is no stereoscopic viewpoint available due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2. The measured parameters may therefore vary slightly.
This measurement is approximate due to lack of visible source location on the disk, and parameters may vary by approximately +/- 10 degrees. Based on best fit in SWPC_CAT.
A very uncertain 3D analysis with two coronagraphs in swpc_cat based on the assumption that the CME is backsided (since we do not see an on-disk source).
Uncertain analysis based on good fit with two coronagraphs in swpc_cat tool.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A to determine the far-sided source likely observed as such in SDO imagery. Also determined to be far-sided by knowing the potential location of old, very active AR 3615 due to return to the E limb in a few days. Longitude range E115-E125.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A to determine the far-sided source likely observed as such in SDO imagery. Also determined to be far-sided by knowing the potential location of old, very active AR 3615 due to return to the E limb in a few days. Longitude range very close to E100.
Measurement only using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 as STEREO COR2A is in a data gap. Using the central source of the dimming (N21W35) for initial parameters and finding a good fit in that range.

Parameters based on SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery. The coronagraph disk's arm in SOHO LASCO C3 obstructs the front of the CME in difference imagery, therefore it was not used in this analysis.
Parameters based on best fit of leading edge in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery.
Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A difference imagery.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event has a diffuse leading edge, which is difficult to measure in later imagery, and exhibits an eastern deflection from the source location. Multiple measurements produced longitudes ranging from -26 to -31 degrees and speeds ranging from 756 to 1064 km/s.

Parameters based on best fit of jet-like feature in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. A faint wider portion is visible in difference imagery, but it is unclear if it is associated with this jet-feature or of it is a separate feature on its own.
Approximate measurement based on source location of flare on or just beyond the east limb.

Measurement using limited imagery due to CME's faintness, based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, primarily focused on the leading edge visible towards the southwest.
Measurement based on best fit in SWPC_CAT. Due to uncertainty, the longitude may vary by approximately +/- 10 degrees.

Measurement based on source beyond limb and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT. Due to lack of clear source location visible on disk, the longitude may vary by approximately +/- 10 degrees.

Measurement based on the approximate source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 there is no stereoscopic viewpoint available to confirm the measurement. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.

Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The leading edge of this event becomes too diffuse to measure in later imagery, so this measurement relies primarily on early frames.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The leading edge becomes diffuse and eventually disappears after a few frames. This measurement relies on early imagery as a result.

Measurement based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 there is no stereoscopic viewpoint available to confirm the measurement. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.

Measurement made using the observed EUV source at S13E70 as a basis, finding a good triangulated fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A imagery very near that lat/lon. Front is not observable far into C3 imagery as it becomes faint.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured parameters likely differ from the source location due to deflection. Multiple measurements produced speeds ranging from 404 to 538 km/s.

Measurement taken using the visible source (S18E75) as the initial parameters and finding a good fit with minimal deflection. Only using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 data as STEREO COR2A is in a data gap. CME quickly fades and is not visible in many C3 frames, only reaching about 8 solar radii in visibility.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The leading edge of this event is diffuse and blends into preceding outflow, making it difficult to measure. Multiple measurements produced speeds ranging from 659 to 759 km/s.

Measurement using the source of AR 3628 (N08W65) as a baseline and finding this to be a good fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, with great imagery up to 19 solar radii. Range could be a few degrees either direction, but quickly loses its strong triangulated fit when >W70.

Measurement made using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, using the observed source at AR 3636 (S21E40) as a basis and finding it to be deflected northwest-ward (making it more Earth-directed) using good triangulation between SOHO and STEREO coronagraphs. The front itself is quite faint and wide, and more visible in STEREO COR2A with its better viewing angle to the side of this particular eruption.

Measurement made using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, using the observed source at AR 3636 (S21E30) as a basis and finding it to be deflected northwest-ward (making it more Earth-directed) using good triangulation between SOHO and STEREO coronagraphs. The front itself is quite faint and wide, and more visible in STEREO COR2A with its better viewing angle to the side of this particular eruption.

This event becomes diffuse and difficult to track in later time stamps, so this measurement relies on early imagery. Additionally, this CME is obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3 so this measurement relies on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Multiple measurements produced longitudes ranging from -90 to -95 degrees.

Measurement based on the source location. The eruption appears to deflect southward, and it is possible the latitude is more southern than the measurement implies. This event becomes diffuse quickly and therefore relies on early imagery. Stereoscopic viewpoints were unavailable for this event due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2.

Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The leading edge of this event becomes diffuse and difficult to track in later imagery, so this measurement relies on early imagery.

Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event partially overlaps with CME: 2024-04-17T02:48Z, making it difficult to determine a precise half-width. Multiple measurements produced speeds ranging from 356 to 757 km/s.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement made using the EUV-observed source at S18E20 as a basis and finding a good fit nearby. Only using SOHO LASCO C2 and the few available C3 frames due to faintness of the front, and lack of usable STEREO COR2A frames due to a data gap.

Measurement made using the center of the EUV-observed source dimming (N01E25) as a basis and finding a good fit with some northern deflection. Only using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 frames with the lack of usable STEREO COR2A frames due to a poor data quality mid-campaign.

Measurement made using the EUV-observed source at S18E16 as a basis, and only SOHO LASCO C2 imagery. The CME is not visible in SOHO LASCO C3 running difference imagery due to the faintness and direction into the pylon, and barely visible if at all in STEREO COR2A due to poor data quality mid-campaign (which has no effect on direct imagery visibility).

Measurement made using the EUV-observed source at S18E12 as a basis and finding a good fit with some southern deflection. Uses all coronagraphs, but only visible in a few frames of SOHO LASCO C3 due to faintness and traveling directly into the pylon.
The longitude was estimated based on the approximate source location. Data gap in STEREO A from 2024-04-18T19:39Z to 2024-04-19T02:23Z.

Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event is partially obscured by the pylon in C3, but the leading edge feature was still mostly visible and able to be tracked in this measurement.

Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. It is difficult to obtain a decent fit without using a southern latitude which deflects greatly from the source location.

Measurement based on the approximate source location. A precise source location is unknown since the eruption occurs slightly beyond the eastern limb, likely around longitude -90 to -100 degrees, so the measured parameters may vary slightly.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The leading edge is diffuse and difficult to measure. This measurement was the best estimate based on the approximate source location, but multiple measurements resulted in speeds ranging from 836 to 940 km/s.
CME was too faint to track well into SOHO LASCO C3. Parameters are based off of the best fit using mostly SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based off of source location and best fit of leading edge feature in SOHO LASCO C2 difference imagery. The feature is too faint to see in C3 difference imagery.
Feature may be slightly wider than this fit but exhibits a dimpled front. The main bulk of this CME was measured within these parameters using SOHO LASCO C2 and C3 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based off of best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery.

Measurement of faint leading edge using source location with heavy deflection to the south and to more central longitudes (corroborated by apparent trajectory of material seen in 304 UV imagers). Data gap in STEREO A COR2 lasting from 2024-04-21T11:38Z to 2024-04-22T02:23Z (with one frame timestamped 2024-04-2119:23Z). No STEREO A imagery was available for this CME.

Measurement using source location with heavy deflection to the south and to more central longitudes (corroborated by apparent trajectory of material seen in 304 UV imagers). Data gap in STEREO A COR2 lasting from 2024-04-21T11:38Z to 2024-04-22T02:23Z (with one frame timestamped 2024-04-2119:23Z). No STEREO A imagery was available for this CME.

Measurement made with only SOHO LASCO C3 imagery, as the CME was deflecting in C2 and not reliably trackable until it settled in the C3 FOV, alongside a data gap in STEREO COR2A. Initial lat/lon were based on the observed EUV source, and the deflection to the south and east was roughly estimated on the behavior of very similar CME 2024-04-22T06:12Z which featured all coronagraphs for triangulation. As such, some variance in the true lat/lon may be present with only one coronagraph measuring this fairly central longitude, with good confidence in the high southern latitude out of the ecliptic plane.

Bulk measurement. Measurement made with only SOHO LASCO C3 imagery, as the CME was deflecting in C2 and not reliably trackable until it settled in the C3 FOV, alongside a data gap in STEREO COR2A. Initial lat/lon were based on the observed EUV source, and the deflection to the south and east was roughly estimated on the behavior of very similar CME 2024-04-22T06:12Z which featured all coronagraphs for triangulation. As such, some variance in the true lat/lon may be present with only one coronagraph measuring this fairly central longitude, with good confidence in the high southern latitude out of the ecliptic plane.

Shock measurement of a wider and faint, but noticeable feature traveling with the bulk front (which tracks with the observed EUV wave in SDO). Made with only SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery due to a data gap in STEREO COR2A, and as such some variance in the true lat/lon may be noted given the single coronagraph measurement. Initial lat/lon were based on the observed EUV source, and found a good fit near this with some southward deflection.

Measurement made based on the EUV observed source and finding a good southward and eastward deflection via triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A. Left-Hand-Boundary is used as the leading edge is traveling a bit askew to the disk due to the observed deflection in EUV and coronagraphs, and was the most reliable way to track the front.

Measurement made with only SOHO LASCO C3 and late STEREO COR2A imagery, as the CME was deflecting in C2 and not reliably trackable until it settled in the C3 FOV. Initial lat/lon were based on the observed EUV source, and the deflection to the south and east was roughly estimated on the behavior of very similar CME 2024-04-22T06:12Z which featured all coronagraphs for triangulation. As such, some variance in the true lat/lon may be present with only one coronagraph measuring this fairly central longitude, with good confidence in the high southern latitude out of the ecliptic plane.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on the approximate longitude of former Active Region 13634. Since this source is far-sided and a precise source location is unknown, this measurement is estimated and the parameters may vary.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The longitude is based on the source location but the source eruption exhibits a clear southern deflection. This matches the southern latitude corresponding to the best fit between coronagraphs.
Measured longitude based on the source location. This event is only visible for a few frames, this measurement relies on early imagery as a result.

Measurement made by fitting narrow flux rope feature seen especially well in STEREO A COR2 difference imagery. Measurement made using source location and trajectory of eruption as seen in UV imagery, which suggests some western deflection, as well as best fit between coronagraphs, which indicated southward deflection.
Measurment of possible SHOCK feature, characterized by fainter wider feature preceding the bulk.

Measured longitude based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event exhibits a northern deflection from the source eruption which is why the measured latitude differs from the source location.
Measurement taken using source location observed in UV imagery and best fit between coronagraph imagery.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measured longitude based on the source location. Other parameters are a result of the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. This event has a diffuse leading edge which becomes difficult to track in later imagery.

Longitude based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. Limited STEREO A COR2 imagery was available for this measurement due to poor difference imagery quality and leading edge faintness.
Limited imagery available for this measurement due to the faintness of the leading edge in difference imagery.
This measurement is uncertain due to the faintness of the leading edge. A range of measurements fit this CME in SWPC_CAT, ranging from approximately 29 to 54 degrees longitude.
Measurement based on source location beyond the limb and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement using limited SOHO LASCO C2/C3. The leading edge of the CME fades away quickly and is only visible in limited early frames of SOHO LASCO C3. Longitude approximated from source location.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Multiple measurements produced speeds ranging from 1074 to 1098 km/s.
Measurement of the CME front before it is overtaken by and potentially merges with CME 2024-04-25T18:24Z, rendering it unseen beyond the first few SOHO LASCO C2 frames.
Measurement taken using source location. Leading edge becomes diffuse after only a handful of frames in C3.
Extremely faint leading edge feature best seen in C3 difference imagery. Measurement based on source location observed in UV imagery.

Measurement based on the source location. The source eruption spans a large area so it is possible the longitude ranges from -50 to -70 degrees. Due to a data gap in STEREO A there is no stereoscopic viewpoint to confirm the measured parameters, so they may vary slightly as a result.

Measurement of a SHOCK-like feature based on source location as seen in UV imagery. This CME is not seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap lasting from 2024-04-27T19:53Z to 2024-04-28T02:23Z.
Measurement of the irregularly shaped BULK feature.
Measurement using best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2, and using farsided coordinates based on souce signatures in UV imagery.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Since the source eruption is seen beyond the limb and a precise location is unknown, the measured longitude may vary slightly.
Measurement based on the approximate location of former Active Region 13645. As the source is far-sided and a precise location is unknown, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
As the source eruption is far-sided, a precise source location is unknown. The longitude was estimated and may vary between 110 to 125 degrees.
A very approximate measurement since the source is not seen on disk and the front of this faint CME in coronagraphs is fuzzy and not clear in most images.
Approximate analysis obtained by getting a good fit of the front in two coronagraphs and confirmed by the location of the post-eruptive arcade/flare in Active Region 3654.
Measurement based on UV signature, candidate farsided source location of old Active Regions, and best fit between coronagraphs.
Analysis based on a good fit in swpc_cat with two coronagraphs and on the source location - flare from Active Region 3657 and associated eruption with dimming to the east of the active region.
This analysis of the faint wider shock of this CME is uncertain as it is based only on the fit of a very faint shock front in two coronagraphs.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery and the source longitude.

Measurement based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2, there are no stereoscopic viewpoints available to confirm the measured parameters. The parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

This measurement is based on the approximate source location which occurs near the western limb. Additionally, there is a data gap in STEREO A COR2 during this event so there is no stereoscopic viewpoint available. As a result, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

Somewhat speculative analysis of the fainter wider front, based on the fit in SOHO LASCO and STEREO COR2 and on the location of the source. Latitude/longtitude and the half width of the fit may vary, so the speed is approximate.

Analysis of the narrower slightly brighter part of the CME which has filamentary structures. It is likely the bulk of the CME (while the wider fainter front is its shock). This analysis is based on the fit between the front seen in STEREO and SOHO coronagraph imagery and indicates a possible deflection of the CME from the location of the flare towards the northeast.

Analysis based on the fit of all three consecutive fronts of this CME with both SOHO and STEREO coronagraphs. These are likely parameters of the shock of this CME. The CME seems to have deflected to the east of the flare/active region location (N26E05) but the ejecta is seen going more towards the east in AIA 304.
Analysis of the likely bulk front of the CME (narrower of two main front-like features).

Measurement using only SOHO LASCO C2 due to data availability, with C3 and STEREO COR2A in a data gap beyond the first few images. Source of N09E33 used as initial location, finding a good visual fit to the northern loop of this pair of fronts (southern loop: CME 2024-05-03T21:28Z) with some slight deflection northward.

Measurement using only SOHO LASCO C2 due to data availability, with C3 and STEREO COR2A in a data gap beyond the first few images. Source of N09E33 used as initial location, finding a good visual fit to the southern loop of this pair of fronts (northern loop: CME 2024-05-03T21:17Z) with some slight deflection southward.

Updated BULK measurement using now available SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery in addition to STEREO COR2A, to include more imagery and better triangulate the lat/lon. Noting some heavy deflection away from the source, but this was the only way triangulation between the coronagraphs would fit.

Measurement of WIDER SHOCK-LIKE FEATURE made with only STEREO COR2A data, as SOHO LASCO C2/C3 data is presently unavailable (further measurements may be made when data available). Source of AR 3663 (N26W09) used as the M9.1 flare is a clear source with some NE deflection observed, and visually fits well in swpc_cat despite the single-coronagraph measurement.
Updated BULK measurement using now available SOHO LASCO C3 imagery in addition to STEREO COR2A, to include more imagery and better triangulate the lat/lon.

Updated BULK measurement using now available SOHO LASCO C3 imagery in addition to STEREO COR2A, to include more imagery and better triangulate the lat/lon. Noting some heavy deflection away from the source (potentially evident in SDO imagery with some field line movement to the NE of the eruption), but this was the only way triangulation between the coronagraphs would fit.
Updated measurement with back-filled SOHO imagery. Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. This event is diffuse and therefore relies on early imagery.

Updated measurement with backfilled SOHO imagery. This measurement is based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Due to the uncertain source location, it is possible the measured parameters vary slightly.

Updated measurement with backfilled SOHO imagery. This measurement is based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Due to the uncertain source location, it is possible the measured parameters vary slightly.

Measurement based on the approximate source location, which exhibits deflection likely due to surrounding coronal holes. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 there is no stereoscopic viewpoint available for this measurement. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
A very speculative final analysis using LASCO imagery only. The CME front is faint and the source - minor flaring and eruptive activity in Active Regions 3664 and 3668 - is tentative.
A very approximate one-coronagraph (SOHO LASCO C2) measurement based on the location of the perceived source (small filament eruption).

More precise analysis made once LASCO imagery became available with two coronagraphs. Analysis is based on the best fit of the CME front with SOHO LASCO and STEREO A and indicates that the CME likely deflected northwards from the source (active region).
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event is faint and difficult to measure due to surrounding outflow/possible merging fronts. Multiple measurements produced speeds ranging from 258 to 381 km/s and longitudes ranging from -17 to -26 degrees.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event is difficult to measure due to how faint the leading edge is.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STREREO A COR2 imagery.
Analysis based on the best fit of the front in two coronagraphs. The fit corresponds well to the location of Active Region 13664 at the time, confirming it as a likely source.
Analysis based on the best fit of the front in two coronagraphs, corresponds well to the location of active region 3664 at the time, confirming it as a source

BULK measurement using both coronagraphs to triangulate the same features, using AR 3664 as the source. This CME front is notably asymmetric in all imagery, but there is high confidence in all three "nodes" in different directions pertaining to the same CME front directed towards Earth from this same eruption. The right hand boundary was mainly used, matching the half-width to the peaks of each node of the asymmetric front, which tracked surprisingly well once aligned.

SHOCK measurement made using both coronagraphs. Source of AR 3664 (S22W10) used as a basis finding a good triangulation between coronagraphs near here, mainly tracking the southern portion of the front as it is the most visible and distinguishable amongst the rest of the complex running difference imagery at this time.

Preliminary BULK measurement made using both coronagraphs, with limited imagery due to a current gap between SOHO downlinks due to resume at T17:15Z today and recency of the front emerging in all imagery. Source of S22W11 (the M8.6-Class flare) used as a basis, finding a good triangulation between SOHO and STEREO with some NE deflection.

Preliminary SHOCK measurement made using both coronagraphs, with limited imagery due to a current gap between SOHO downlinks due to resume at T17:15Z today and recency of the front emerging in all imagery. Source of S22W11 (the M8.6-Class flare) used as a basis finding a good triangulation between coronagraphs near here.
Uses source location and best stereoscopic fit. Only two frames of STEREO A COR2 difference imagery were used, due to a data gap lasting from 2024-05-08T19:53Z to 2024-05-09T01:53Z.

Measurement of the SHOCK using the source location seen in UV imagery. Only C3 difference imagery used. No imagery available from STEREO A COR2 due to data gap lasting from 2024-05-08T19:53Z to 2024-05-09T01:53Z.

Measurement of the BULK using the source location seen in UV imagery. Only C3 difference imagery used. No imagery available from STEREO A COR2 due to data gap lasting from 2024-05-08T19:53Z to 2024-05-09T01:53Z.
These parameters are based on the best fit of the BULK of the CME. Measured speeds varied between 1200km/s to 1500km/s.
SHOCK parameters based off of best fit in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Analysis of the slightly brighter, mostly western bulk front of this CME based on the location of the flare and dimming and on the good fit with two coronagraphs.
Analysis of the fainter full halo shock front, made by fitting this front in two coronagraphs.
Very speculative analysis based on a fit with two coronagraphs. The source could be much further behind the limb.

Bulk measurement based on the brightest, inner feature. Measured parameters based on the source eruption and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. Multiple bulk measurements produced speeds ranging from 1018 to 1133 km/s and longitudes spanning from 31 to 37 degrees.

Measurement of the shock portion which extends towards the south and east compared to the bulk. Measured parameters based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2, which deviates east of the source location.

Analysis is based on a good fit of the narrow CME front in two coronagraphs and is loosely supported by the location of the gradually erupting filament, however this source is tenuous because the final eruption of the filament seems to happen after the start of the CME at 2024-05-10T21:28Z.
Analysis with one coronagraph only (C2), with a default half width, so this analysis is very speculative.

Updated bulk parameters. The CME bulk appears to have an earlobe shape to it with a smoother loop towards the north and a messy front to the south. It is possible these fronts could be interpreted as two separate CMEs overlapping each other, however they travel together in white-light and difference imagery.

This is a SHOCK measurement based on the best fit of shock features in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. Due to the asymmetric shape of the shock front, this measurement leaves out a little bit of the north/northwestern portion of the shock front.

Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. There seems to be some northward deflection since although the longitude of the fit corresponds well to the longitude of the Active Region 3664 at the time, the latitude of the fit is more northern than the latitude of the region.

Update measurement of the BULK now that more imagery has become available after the SOHO LASCO downlink. The parameters are similar to the preliminary measurement, but the speed is slower now that more frames have been included in this measurement.
Measurement of BULK using source location observed in UV imagery on the SW limb and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, using a farsided source location based on source signatures seen in UV imagery.
Measurement using farsided source parameters based on source signatures in UV imagery, as well as best 3D fit between coronagraphs.
Measurement with both coronagraphs to triangulate a good fit given the uncertain, high latitude source of this eruption.
Measurement of the BULK using source location seen in UV imagery, which also indicates some southern deflection, and best stereoscopic fit between coronagraphs.
Measurement of the SHOCK of the CME using best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs.
Measurement made using the source of the X1.7 flare on or very near the limb, could be W85-W95. Triangulated well between SOHO and STEREO at longitude W95, but could vary some.

Measurement at E90, not as plane of sky, but for good triangulation between SOHO and STEREO coronagraphs. Initial potential source of AR 3679 (S09E60) does not fit too well, but E90-E100 fit much nicer between the coronagraphs, indicating a potential source on/just beyond the SE limb.
Measurement using the central source of the dimming ~N25E35 as a start, finding a good triangulated fit between SOHO and STEREO imagery with some northward deflection.
Used approximate location of source signature as seen in UV imagery, used candidate source location AR 13664 approximated longitude of W90-W95, and best fit between coronagraphs.

Measured at W90 not as a plane-of-sky, but an average longitude as the source could range W85-W95 with the X1.2 flare occurring on or very near the limb. Triangulates well between SOHO and STEREO imagery at these longitudes.

Analysis based on the fit in two coronagraph and on the longitude of the associated significant flare. We do not see the flare source since it is behind the limb so the exact location of the eruption could be somewhat different from the calculated location of the active region behind the limb.
Analysis based on the fit of the faint front in coronagraph and is tentative.
This analysis is based on the calculated longitude of the Active Region 3664, which has rotated behind the west limb.
Measurement using the source of AR 3682 (N18E72) and finding a great triangulation between SOHO and STEREO coronagraphs almost exactly at this point.
Measurement of the BULK using candidate source location AR 13664 approximated longitude of W105, and best fit between coronagraphs.
Measurement of the BULK using candidate source location AR 13664 approximated longitude of W105 and best fit between coronagraphs, finding a good triangulation deflected to W114.
Measurement using best fit between C3 and STEREO A COR2 frames and inference that the source location is farsided based on UV imagery.
Measurement made by triangulating available SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery with STEREO COR2A using the broad, on-disk source centered ~N35E35 as a basis.
Used approximate location of source signature as seen in UV imagery, used candidate source location AR 13664 approximated longitude of W105, and best fit between coronagraphs.
Measurement using the X2.9 flare from AR 3685 on or just beyond the SE limb as the source, finding a decent fit between STEREO COR2A and the limited available SOHO LASCO C3 imagery.
Measurement of the leading edge using approximate location of AR 3664. No STEREO A COR2 imagery was available for this measurement.
Parameters obtained based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT, using potential source location beyond limb. The longitude may vary by approx. +/- 10 degrees.
Parameters obtained based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT, using potential source location beyond limb. The longitude may vary by approx. +/- 10 degrees.
Parameters based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT, based on possible backside source location.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as potential source location.
Parameters based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Final analysis with two coronagraphs now that SOHO LASCO imagery is available.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs.
Specialtive analysis as no clear source for this CME found on the Earth-facing disk.
Analysis based on a fit with two coronagraphs.
Analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs and on the location of the possible source (filament eruption).
Analysis of the bulk based on the fit in two coronagraphs and confirmed by the calculated current farsided longitude of the former Active Region 13664.
Analysis of the shock of this CME based on the fit in two coronagraphs and confirmed by the calculated farsided longitude of former Active Region 13664.
Analysis based on the source location confirmed by the fit in two coronagraphs.
Measurement of irregular and faint leading edge using a farsided longitude and best fit between coronagraph imagery.
Measurement made using location of source as seen in UV imagery (S11E65) and best fit of bulk portion as seen in C2 and C3.

The exact longitude is uncertain, though believed to be farsided based on UV imagery. Measurement taken using best fit between available SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Imagery of this CME was affected by the reduction in LASCO cadence due to scheduled calibration maneuvers.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO and STEREO COR2A, using the rough center of the filament eruption ~S40E10 as an initial source, finding some southward deflection which may also be observed in AIA imagery.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO and STEREO COR2A using AR 3685 as an initial source, finding a good nearby fit. In the already low amount of available imagery, the front is notably faint and blurry, which made for difficulty tracking the leading edge.
Parameters obtained using source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Limited imagery was available for this measurement due to the CME's faintness.
Measurement of what was determined to likely be the SHOCK of this CME using backsided source and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement of the bulk of this CME using best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Reanalysis with more precise source location.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs and on the source location.
Analysis is based on the fit with two coronagraphs. It is slightly uncertain since the CME seems to be much more southward than the flare.

Uncertain analysis based on the fit in coronagraph. The CME seems more southwestern than the location of the post-eruptive arcades, but it corresponds well to the latitude of the erupting filament seen in AIA 304.

Measurement made as a triangulation between SOHO and STEREO. Notably difficult capture of the leading edge due to the southward deflection (observed in GOES SUVI 284), which misaligned the CME travel direction from the measurement tool connected to disk center. A best fit is done with low confidence, but there is high confidence in this CME being out of the ecliptic regardless.

Measurement made with only SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery, as STEREO COR2A is in a data gap and did not observe this front. Source of AR 3579 used (S06W47) as a basis, and finding the longitude fit with the observed EUV northward deflection. Some variance could exist in the longitude given the one-spacecraft measurement.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Since this event is far-sided and there is no source location available, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO and STEREO COR2A using the M1.5 flare from AR 3679 (S06W52) as an initial source, and finding a fit at this longitude with northward deflection that was observed in SDO imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is far-sided and there is no precise source location, the measured parameters vary slightly.
Analysis based on fit in two coronagraphs.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Analysis based on the location of the associated flare and confirmed by the fit in two coronagraphs.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LACSO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event is mostly obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3 imagery and becomes too diffuse to measure after a few frames. This measurement relies on early imagery as a result.
Analysis based on the location of the associated flare and confirmed by the fit in two coronagraphs.
Analysis based on the location of the associated flare and confirmed by the fit in two coronagraphs.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event becomes diffuse and difficult to track after a few frames, so this measurement relies on early imagery.

Measurement based on the source location. The measured parameters may vary slightly as there is no stereoscopic viewpoint available to confirm the measurement due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 at this time.
Measured longitude based on the source location. The measured parameters may vary slightly due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery which occurs during this event.
Very approximate analysis based on fit with two coronagraphs. No source was found on disk or on limb.

Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A at this time, there is no stereoscopic viewpoint available to confirm the measured parameters. They may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement based on the approximate source location. The measured parameters may vary slightly as there is no STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event due to a data gap.
Analysis based on fit with two coronagraphs, indicating that the source longitude is higher than estimated (behind the limb).
Analysis is based on the fit with two coronagraphs and confirmed by the location of the associated flare.
These parameters are based off of the best fit of the CME in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery.

Parameters are based on the best fit of the BULK of the CME. The CME exhibits a dimpled front and continues to expand in width as it progresses out of the field of view. Most of the CME's bulk is captured in this analysis, however portions on the left hand boundary were excluded in later frames due to the expansion of the CME.

Parameters based on the SHOCK of the CME. Due to the irregular shape of the CME and shock, some portions of the shock were excluded to attempt a best fit. Parameters may vary due to limitations with measuring techniques.
Very speculative analysis based on the fit in two coronagraphs. There is no clear source and the fit can have a range of longitudes, at least from 100 deg to 130 deg.

Very speculative analysis based on the fit in two coronagraphs. The fit can have a range of longitudes, at least from 105 deg to 140 deg. The CME's coronal signatures were interpreted as indicating a more backsided source of 135 deg, however this is subjective. No clear backsided Active Region candidate has been found for this event (former Active Region 3683 which is at the longitude of 170 degrees) is not a candidate for this event based on the fit.
Speculative analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. The source is backsided and the CME is almost completely covered by the nighttime data gap in STEREO coronagraph.

An extremely speculative analysis based on one fit with two coronagraphs. No source has been found for this CME and the front is very faint in both coronagraphs. Unfortunately, fits with two coronagraph are possible with a wide range of longitudes.
A very uncertain analysis based on the fit of this very faint halo in one coronagraph (LASCO) with very few timestamps.
Analysis based on the location of the source (close to the east limb) and confirmed by the fit in two coronagraphs.
Parameters of the SHOCK of the CME based on SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery.
This CME appears to have three parts, a shock, bulk/leading edge, and a core/trailing edge. These parameters are based on the potential BULK of the CME.

This CME appears to have three parts, a shock, bulk/leading edge, and a core/trailing edge. These parameters are based on the CORE/trailing edge of the CME which appears as the brightest portion in white-light imagery the longest.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 at the time of this event, there is no stereoscopic viewpoint available and the measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event becomes diffuse after early imagery, and this measurement relies on early frames as a result.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement using fit between coronagraphs.
Measured using best fit.

Somewhat uncertain analysis of the outer, earlier front of this halo CME (possibly its shock), based on the fit with two coronagraphs. Only one coronagraph frame is available for STEREO A COR2 bc of a data gap, adding to the uncertainty of analysis. These parameters are somewhat confirmed by the location of a large backsided active region in helioseismology imagery from GONG which is likely the former AR 13679 that was at lat/lon -8/-170 at the time of event.

Approximate analysis of the second front of this halo CME (possibly its bulk), based on a fit with two coronagraphs. For STEREO A COR2 imagery only one timestamp is available (bc of a large data gap), adding to the uncertainty of analysis. Analysis is supported by the location of a large backsided active region in helioseismology imagery from GONG - likely the former AR 13679 that was around lat/lon -8/-170 at the time of this event. In addition, STIX instrument on Solar Orbiter detected a significant flare at 2024-06-01T18:45Z.

A very uncertain analysis is based on the source location of the dimming associated with the flare and on the fit with one coronagraph (SOHO) since in STEREO A COR2 the CME is fully covered by the night time data gap. The shape of the CME front, as it is seen overlapping with the preceding, likely backsided, halo, is very complex and the exact width is hard to determine. There also can be a range of possible longitudes (at least from -20 to -25 degrees), from the longitude of the associated flare to the more eastern longitude of the associated dimming.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs.

Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event become diffuse in later imagery and therefore relies on earlier frames for the measurement.
One-coronagraph measurement with SOHO C2 images only, based on the location of Active Region 3695 which produced the associated flare and ejecta.
Measurement of slightly asymmetrical leading edge.

Measured longitude based on the approximate source location. The measured parameters may vary slightly as no stereoscopic viewpoint was available due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 at the time of this event.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is far-sided and a precise source location is unknown, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The leading edge of this event becomes diffuse and difficult to measure in later imagery. This measurement relies on earlier imagery as a result.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is far-sided and a precise source location is unknown, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
Measurement of faint leading edge using source location and best fit between coronagraphs. Leading edge becomes too diffuse to track prior to reaching halfway into STEREO A COR2 field of view.

BULK measurement made with more complete SOHO frames in LASCO C2/C3 following its downlink backfill. Very little has changed between the preliminary analysis, finding a good triangulation between SOHO and STEREO coronagraphs here.

SHOCK measurement made with more complete SOHO frames in LASCO C2/C3 following its downlink backfill. This shock front is dimpled between two apparent fronts, seen in both SOHO and STEREO coronagraphs, which made for some difficulty triangulating the two images. Eventually settled on a triangulation using the top of the halo shock as the shared feature between both, finding a good fit.
Measurement based on the approximate source location. This event becomes too diffuse to measure after a few frames and the measurement relies on early imagery as a result.
Measurement based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A, there is no stereoscopic viewpoint available to confirm the measured parameters. They may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraph imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event disappears after a few frames and the measurement relies on early imagery as a result.

Measured longitude based on the approximate source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 at the time of this event, there is no stereoscopic viewpoint available. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Analysis based on the fit in two coronagraphs and on the source location of the associated flare.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs and on the location of the associated flare.
Very approximate analysis of the likely more backsided shock based on the best fit with two coronagraphs only.
Measurement made using only SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and the source region to start, finding southward deflection. Heavy overlap with a much wider CME erupting shortly after this leading edge is observed.
BULK measurement using only SOHO C3 imagery, due to a STEREO COR2A data gap. Source longitude of AR 3697 (S18W96) used as a basis, finding a northward deflection with a good fit.
SHOCK measurement using only SOHO C3 imagery, due to a STEREO COR2A data gap. Source longitude of AR 3697 (S18W96) used as a basis, ultimately fitting much better around W139.

Measurement made by triangulation between SOHO and STEREO, finding deflection away from the source centered around N32E55, but this deflection northwestward is observed during the rising loop eruption in SDO AIA 171.

Measurement of the two apparent fronts from this eruption as one, requiring a right hand boundary measurement. Source initially appeared as the far-sided filament closer to the limb, perhaps W100-W110, but ultimately could not fit a triangulation between SOHO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A unless we went W130-W145.
Measurement based on source location with adjustment for best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Approximate measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT. Due to the uncertainty in the source location of this measurement, the longitude may vary by approximately +/- 10 degrees.
UPDATED BULK measurement using source location approx. longitude of W112, several frames of C3, and the one frame of STEREO A COR2 that had some indication of the leading edge in the frame.

Parameters based on the BULK of the CME and potential source of AR13697 based on solar rotation calculation and visual inspection of source from EUV imagery. Longitude may vary due to lack of stereoscopic viewpoints of CME and source.

Parameters based on best fit of the SHOCK of CME using potential source of AR13697 (based on solar calculation). Portions of the CME shock to the north and south of the CME were excluded due to measurement limitations.
The measured longitude is speculative due to the far-sided and uncertain source location. The measured parameters were based on the best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and may vary.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR 2 imagery. The measured parameters may vary slightly as the source is far-sided and there is no precise source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured longitude may vary as there is no precise source location due to the far-sided source.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured longitude is loosely based on the approximate location of former Active Region 13697 based on the rate of solar rotation.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. The measured longitude may vary slightly as the precise source location is unknown.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The leading edge becomes diffuse and difficult to track in later imagery, so this measurement relies primarily on early frames.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT. This best fit is also supported by preliminary measurement in StereoCAT.

Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT. The CME fades away rapidly in the field of view of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, with possible deflection in the coronagraphs from the source eruption.

Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, with slight deflection from source location. This measurement focuses on the brightest leading edge of the CME. In later frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2, a fainter, possible shock front is visible ahead of this leading edge, which was also analyzed and determined to have a similar speed.
Parameters obtained using best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 due to lack of source location.
Measurement of the leading edge using the best fit between an abundance of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 frames. Source location is farsided as indicated by UV imagery.
Measurement of bulk portion using best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and using a source location beyond the limb, as indicated by UV imagery.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement of leading edge using best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 CME in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Longitude based on potential source location. Measurement of the brighter leading edge, not the fainter material that was not clearly measurable in SOHO LASCO C3.
Longitude approximated from source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. Limited C3 imagery was available for this measurement due to the CME's faintness.
Limited imagery was available for this measurement due to the CME's faintness.

A range of longitudes were obtained in SWPC_CAT, ranging from -122 to -144. Latitudes ranged from -44 to -60. This was deemed to be the best measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Initial fit made using the on-disk filament source alongside a triangulated fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and the limited STEREO COR2A frames available. Due to observed acceleration while measuring, only C3 frames are used for this measurement once the speed stabilized. There is significant variance possible in this measurement, with the two filaments erupting simultaneously and blending into this singular observed front.
Measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only due to the faintness of the leading edge in STEREO A imagery.
Measurement of diffuse leading edge using best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. The CME may deflect to the east of the source location.

Measurement made using the source of AR 3711 (S12W76) and triangulation between SOHO and STEREO coronagraphs, finding a great fit near this source. This fit is of the more northern of the two fronts sourced from this same eruption.

Measurement made using the source of AR 3711 (S12W76) and triangulation between SOHO and STEREO coronagraphs, finding a great fit near this source. This fit is of the more southern of the two fronts sourced from this same eruption.
Approximate measurement based on the fit with two coronagraphs. Potential source (eruption off/behind the SE limb) is super minor.

Measurement is based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Parameters may vary slightly as the source of this CME is likely backsided, leading to the lack of a precise source location.

Approximate analysis based on the assumption that the source is behind the limb and on a good fit with two coronagraphs. The longitude could vary by +- 10 degrees (to produce a fit with two coronagraphs).
Analysis based on the fit in two coronagraphs, corroborated by the location of the likely source (Active Region 13719).

Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT, taking into account potential source eruption beyond east limb. Due to the uncertainty of this measurement, due to lack of visible source location on disk, the longitude may vary by approximately +/- 10 degrees.

Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT. Due to lack of source location in available EUV imagery, this measurement is approximate, and the longitude may vary by approximately +/- 10 degrees.

Measurement of the inner, bright bulk portion based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured longitude was loosely based on the approximate longitude of former Active Region 13697.
Measurement of the fainter shock feature based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is far-sided and a precise location is unknown, the measured longitude may vary slightly.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Because the source is far-sided and a precise source location is unknown, the measured longitude may vary slightly.

Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The leading edge was difficult to track as it overlapped and merged slightly with CME: 2024-06-23T07:48Z and surrounding outflow.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The leading edge of this event was difficult to track as it overlapped with surrounding outflow and the leading edge of CME: 2024-06-23T07:00Z.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Because the source is far-sided and a precise source location is unknown, the measured longitude may vary slightly.

Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs and confirmed by the possible source - a minor slowly developing dimming at (S40E20) in AIA 193 around noon, corroborating the lat/lon derived by fitting with two coronagraphs perfectly. The CME is probably associated with an eruption of one of the narrow filaments stretching from SE to NW.
